intro: Ser, ir, and ver are the three main irregular verbs in the imperfect tense. Use them to describe what someone was like, where someone used to go, and what someone was seeing or watching in the past.

overview.body: In the imperfect, ser becomes era, ir becomes iba, and ver becomes veía. These forms are common in simple descriptions, repeated past movement, and past scenes.
overview.use_note: descriptions, repeated past movement, and past viewing

examples.spanish: Cuando era niño, iba al parque.
examples.english: When I was a child, I used to go to the park.

structure.translation: We used to go to school.

conjugation.subject: Tú
conjugation.translation: You used to go to the club.

conjugation.subject: Él / Ella / Usted
conjugation.translation: She used to see her neighbors.

uses.heading: Descriptions With Ser
uses.body: Use ser in the imperfect to describe what people, places, or situations were like in the past.

examples.spanish: La casa era pequeña pero cómoda.
examples.english: The house was small but comfortable.

uses.heading: Repeated Movement With Ir
uses.body: Use ir in the imperfect to talk about where someone used to go or was going regularly.

examples.spanish: Yo iba a la biblioteca los sábados.
examples.english: I used to go to the library on Saturdays.

examples.spanish: Mis padres iban al trabajo temprano.
examples.english: My parents used to go to work early.

uses.heading: Seeing Or Watching With Ver
uses.body: Use ver in the imperfect for what someone was seeing, used to see, or was watching in the past.

examples.spanish: Nosotros veíamos dibujos animados después de clase.
examples.english: We used to watch cartoons after class.

examples.spanish: Desde la ventana, ella veía el mar.
examples.english: From the window, she could see the sea.

affirmative.spanish: Yo era muy curioso de niño.
affirmative.english: I was very curious as a child.

affirmative.spanish: Tú ibas a la playa cada verano.
affirmative.english: You used to go to the beach every summer.

affirmative.spanish: Ella veía una serie por la noche.
affirmative.english: She used to watch a series at night.

negative.spanish: Yo no iba al gimnasio antes.
negative.english: I did not use to go to the gym before.

negative.spanish: Él no veía la televisión durante la semana.
negative.english: He did not watch television during the week.

negative.spanish: Ellas no iban a esa escuela.
negative.english: They did not use to go to that school.

questions.english: Were you shy when you were little?
Did you use to go to the park after class?
Did she use to watch movies on Fridays?

select.answer: Tú ibas al parque los domingos.
Ellos iban a la escuela juntos.
Ustedes veían el partido en casa.

complete.prompt: Ella ___ la ciudad desde el balcón.
complete.answer: Ella veía la ciudad desde el balcón.
Ellos iban al cine cada mes.

complete.prompt: Ustedes ___ el mar desde el hotel.
complete.answer: Ustedes veían el mar desde el hotel.

complete.prompt: Nosotras no ___ al centro de noche.
complete.answer: Nosotras no íbamos al centro de noche.

yes_no.answer: Sí, iba a la biblioteca los sábados.
No, nosotros no íbamos al centro.

translate.prompt: You used to go to the park.
They used to go to school.
You all could see the mountains.
Did you use to go every day?

select: Tú ibas al parque los domingos.
Ellos iban a la escuela juntos.
Ustedes veían el partido en casa.
complete: Ella veía la ciudad desde el balcón.
Ellos iban al cine cada mes.
Ustedes veían el mar desde el hotel.
Nosotras no íbamos al centro de noche.
yes_no: Sí, iba a la biblioteca los sábados.
No, nosotros no íbamos al centro.

wrap_up.translation: I used to go to the market on Mondays.
We used to watch movies at home.

wrap_up.example: Era niño e iba al parque.
wrap_up.translation: I was a child and I used to go to the park.
